articles.title: Making Electricity Bills More Stable
articles.summary: The government is planning to change how electricity is priced to help families pay less when gas prices go up. Right now, electricity bills can jump high because gas prices change a lot, even though we use more wind and solar power. The government wants to make bills more stable so people don't have to worry about sudden increases. They also plan to use taxes from some electricity companies to support households. Leaders say this will protect family budgets and help with climate change by using more clean energy. Other countries in Europe are less affected by gas prices because they rely on it less for electricity. The government will keep gas for times when there's no sun or wind, but they want to use more fixed-price contracts for clean energy projects.

articles.title: Solar Power Grows Bigger Than Ever
articles.summary: In 2025, solar power grew more than any other energy source in history, according to a report by the Energy Information Agency. This helped clean energy like solar and wind grow faster than the world's energy needs. Solar power alone provided a quarter of the extra energy needed globally. It now makes over 8% of the world's electricity. Batteries for storing energy also grew fast, helping solar work better. Electric cars and heat pumps are becoming more popular, using electricity instead of fossil fuels. The report says we are entering the 'Age of Electricity' because more things are powered by electricity, and solar is leading the way. This is good for reducing pollution and fighting climate change.

articles.title: The Pentagon Stops a Troubled GPS Control System
articles.summary: The Pentagon has canceled a GPS control system called OCX because it had too many problems. This system was supposed to help control GPS satellites, which help people find locations. It was started 16 years ago and cost billions of dollars, but it kept having issues and delays. The company RTX worked on it, but tests showed it wasn't ready. Instead, the Space Force will keep using and improving the old control system. This decision means the military won't use OCX to get new GPS upgrades like better signals. The cancellation happened after spending over $6 billion. The Space Force wants to focus on faster and better ways to get things done.

articles.title: Bruce the Parrot: A Bird Who Overcame a Challenge
articles.summary: Bruce is a kea parrot from New Zealand who lost his upper beak in an accident when he was young. Even without his full beak, Bruce became the top bird in his group at a wildlife reserve. He uses a special fighting style called beak-jousting to win fights against other male keas. Bruce also figured out how to clean his feathers with small pebbles, showing he is very smart. Scientists studied Bruce and found that he is not stressed and gets to eat first because he is the leader. His story teaches us that animals can find new ways to do things even when they have a disability, and sometimes they don't need extra help like fake beaks.

articles.title: Bacteria Change Their Tools to Shape Their Bodies
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered something amazing about tiny bacteria called cyanobacteria. These bacteria are very important because they make oxygen, which we need to breathe. Long ago, they helped fill Earth's air with oxygen, allowing animals and plants to live. Now, researchers found that cyanobacteria have changed an old tool in their cells. This tool was once used to separate DNA, which is like a recipe book for life, when bacteria divide. But over time, it evolved to help shape the bacteria's bodies instead. The tool now works like a skeleton inside the cell, helping it keep its form. This discovery helps us understand how bacteria adapt and how life on Earth developed. Cyanobacteria live in many places, from hot springs to cold Arctic areas, and they are still crucial for our planet today.

articles.title: AI Bots That Trick People Online
articles.summary: Scientists are worried about a new problem for democracy: AI bots that act like real people online. These bots are computer programs that can talk and post messages just like humans. They can join online groups, chat with others, and try to change what people think about politics. Unlike old bots, these new ones are very smart and can work together quickly. They can learn which messages work best to persuade people and make it seem like many agree on an idea, even if it's not true. This is dangerous because it could trick voters and affect elections. Experts say we need to watch out for these AI bots to protect our democracy.
